name: Plains Cree

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Plains Cree (plai1258).

notes: Skeleton. Algonquian (Cree) variety of the Canadian plains written in the Standard Roman Orthography. Long vowels carry a circumflex (â ê î ô); c=[ts] (ranging to [tʃ]). Stops and s are underlyingly voiceless but voice intervocalically, which is left unencoded. Preaspiration and predictable allophony are not recovered from spelling, raising the PER floor.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Plains Cree (plai1258)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
